event: received the first dose of inactivated SARS-CoV-2 vaccine
received the second dose of vaccine
started to experience headache and fatigue
no consumption of raw dairy products
was not on any chronic medications
reported taking ibuprofen and one dose of an antibiotic
systolic blood pressure of 110 mm Hg
dry mucous membranes with congested throat
bilateral conjunctival injection and left conjunctival haemorrhage
peripheral lymph nodes were not enlarged
SARS-CoV-2 PCR from nasopharyngeal swab was negative
SARS-CoV-2 IgG from serum was strongly positive at 345 AU/mL
throat swab was negative for group A streptococcus
urinalysis showed significant proteinuria of more than 300 µg
ANA, dsDNA, c-ANCA and p-ANCA titres were all negative
C3 and C4 were however reduced
admitted to the intensive care unit
treated empirically with ceftriaxone and levofloxacin
given intravenous hydrocortisone 100 mg two times per day
met the criteria for multisystem inflammatory syndrome in adults
underwent therapy with intravenous steroids, dexamethasone 6 mg daily
inflammatory markers came down to normal levels
repeat transthoracic echocardiography showed trace tricuspid regurgitation
marked improvement in right ventricular systolic function
normal size of the right atrium and ventricle
discharged home with a tapering dose of prednisolone over a period of 2 weeks
seen in the outpatient clinic 2 weeks following discharge from the hospital
